item.title: EV startup Harbinger reveals a smaller work truck with electric and hybrid variants
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The LA-based startup is trying to corner the commercial truck market with its electric and hybrid trucks that offer a lower cost of ownership and a better driving experience.

item.title: Google wraps up $32B acquisition of cloud cybersecurity startup Wiz
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Google has officially acquired Israeli cybersecurity firm Wiz for $32 billion in all-cash, a full year after the companies announced the deal. This marks Google's biggest acquisition in its history.

item.title: Health insurance startup Alan reaches €5B valuation
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: French health insurance startup Alan is now valued at €5 billion — approximately $5.83 billion, up from $4.5 billion in 2024.

item.title: In a vote of confidence for Meta’s Threads, Kalshi adds sharing feature
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Kalshi now offers a share option that will automatically embed the relevant prediction market chart into a Threads post.

item.title: Mandiant’s founder just raised $190M for his autonomous AI agent security startup
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Kevin Mandia founded Armadin to create autonomous cybersecurity agents, software designed to learn and respond to threats without a human in the middle.

item.title: Legora reaches $5.55 billion valuation as AI legal tech boom endures
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Legora, an AI platform for lawyers, is now valued at $5.55 billion following a $550 million Series D led by Accel to fuel its growth in the U.S.

item.title: Hyperscale Power is the latest startup to challenge 140-year-old transformer tech
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Startup Hyperscale Power is developing technology that promises to shrink power transformers, freeing up precious space within data centers.

item.title: Sandbar secures $23M Series A for its AI note-taking ring
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Sandbar aims to ship the Stream, which can be used to take notes, chat with an AI assistant, and for media playback, this summer.

item.title: AI network startup Eridu emerges from stealth with hefty $200M Series A
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Unlike a vibe-coded product built by a college dropout, Eridu's co-founder, Drew Perkins, has been inventing networking tech since the dawn of the internet.

item.title: Uzbekistan’s Uzum valuation leaps over 50% in 7 months to $2.3B
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Uzum now reaches 20 million users and processes $11 billion in payments as it expands its e-commerce and fintech ecosystem across Uzbekistan.

item.title: Yann LeCun’s AMI Labs raises $1.03B to build world models
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: “My prediction is that ‘world models’ will be the next buzzword,” AMI Labs CEO Alexandre LeBrun told TechCrunch. “In six months, every company will call itself a world model to raise funding.”

item.title: Bluesky CEO Jay Graber steps down
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Graber explained that, as a more mature company, Bluesky needs a "seasoned operator focused on scaling and execution." Graber said she feels better suited to building Bluesky's technology itself.

item.title: Periwinkle is making self-hosted social media on Bluesky’s AT Protocol even easier
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Periwinkle offers managed hosting for AT Protocol users who want social media accounts on their own domains, with backups, storage, and migration tools.

item.title: Palmer Luckey’s retro gaming startup ModRetro reportedly seeks funding at $1B valuation
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The company launched its first product, a Game Boy-style handheld device called the Chromatic, in 2024.

item.title: Robinhood’s startup fund stumbles in NYSE debut
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The fund currently offers retail investors exposure to eight startups, including Mercor, Ramp, and Stripe, with plans to expand its portfolio.

item.title: Anthropic’s Pentagon deal is a cautionary tale for startups chasing federal contracts
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: The Pentagon has officially designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the two failed to agree on how much control the military should have over its AI models, including its use in autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance. As Anthropic’s $200 million contract fell apart, the DoD turned to OpenAI instead, which accepted and then watched ChatGPT uninstalls surge 295%. As the stakes keep rising, the question remains: how much unrestricted […]

item.title: Anthropic vs. the Pentagon, the SaaSpocalypse, and why competition is good, actually
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo, Kirsten Korosec, Anthony Ha, Sean O'Kane
item.description: The Pentagon has officially designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the two failed to agree on how much control the military should have over its AI models, including its use in autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance. As Anthropic’s $200 million contract fell apart, the DoD turned to OpenAI instead, which accepted and then watched ChatGPT uninstalls surge 295%. As the stakes keep rising, the question remains: how much unrestricted […]

item.title: City Detect, which uses AI to help cities stay safe and clean, raises $13M Series A
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: City Detect, a company that helps local governments prevent urban decay, is in at least 17 cities so far, including Dallas and Miami.

item.title: DiligenceSquared uses AI, voice agents to make M&A research affordable
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Instead of relying on expensive management consultants, the startup uses AI voice agents to conduct interviews with customers of the companies the PE firms are considering buying.

item.title: Cluely CEO Roy Lee admits to publicly lying about revenue numbers last year
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: The $7 million in annual recurring revenue that Cluely CEO Roy Lee shared last summer was a lie, its founder and CEO Roy Lee admitted on Thursday on X.
